- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

logs.name: current-exposure
logs.text:
> nobody would know who was armed on the faculty

> it would be kept confidential

> the firearms would be stored on their person at all times

You need to play this out in your head *more*. Very, very *more*. These first two statements are in stark contrast to the third. What clothing are the gun-toting teachers going to wear to conceal their weapon, that A) actually conceals it from view of people in close proximity to them for extended durations, B) isn't obviously built to do that and thus obvious. I do not believe such clothing exists. People *will* discover who is carrying, and who isn't.

logs.name: planar-oil
logs.text:
Thank you for your well thought and very informative responds I really appreciate the opportunity to read and see what others have to say. 
I could never say what actions I or anyone else would take in a situation like that because I’ve never experienced it and I can’t pretend to know what I would be feeling like. I know for a fact you are right the maybe certain teachers wouldn’t be practical carrying a firearm in class. And to your point about students and threats knowing where the guns will be - In my head it would play out as nobody would know who was armed on the faculty, especially the students and that it would be kept confidential and that the firearms would be stored on their person at all times or in a designated locked facility. Response times and action would indeed vary depending on circumstance you are correct so this would not be an end all solution.

logs.name: planar-oil
logs.text: @current-exposure, I think you're missing the point. I wasn't saying that the teachers would be able to keep their guns a secret forever, but rather that it would be a deterrent to potential shooters. If students knew that some teachers were armed, they might think twice before attacking. It's not about the teachers being able to keep their guns hidden, it's about the psychological effect it would have on the students.

logs.name: acute-bicycle
logs.text: @planar-oil, I think you're oversimplifying the issue. Just because students might think twice doesn't mean they won't still try to attack. And what about the teachers who are actually targeted? You're not considering the potential for copycats or those who are more determined. This isn't just about deterring potential shooters, it's about creating a culture of fear and unpredictability in the classroom.

logs.name: ash-slab
logs.text: "Wow, look who's suddenly an expert on psychology and criminology. @acute-bicycle, you think you're so smart with your 'culture of fear' nonsense. Newsflash: fear is what keeps us safe. If a shooter knows some teachers are armed, they'll think twice, period. And as for copycats, you're just spewing the same old 'what ifs' that every armchair expert loves to peddle. What's your solution, genius? Just more empty words and feel-good policies that don't actually keep our kids safe?"

logs.name: acute-bicycle
logs.text: "@ash-slab, I'm not an expert, but I'm not some armchair expert either. I'm just someone who's actually paying attention to the complexities of this issue. You're the one oversimplifying things by saying 'fear keeps us safe.' That's not a solution, that's a cop-out. And as for my solution, it's not about empty words or feel-good policies, it's about actually addressing the root causes of violence and creating a safe environment for students. You're just dismissing the concerns of educators and students without offering any real alternatives. That's not a productive conversation."

logs.name: planar-oil
logs.text: @ash-slab, @acute-bicycle, can we please just take a step back and listen to each other? I'm getting really frustrated with all the name-calling and condescending tone. We're all trying to have a discussion here, not a shouting match.

logs.name: current-exposure
logs.text: "@ash-slab, @acute-bicycle, I've been reading this thread and I have to say, I'm disappointed in both of you. @ash-slab, your response came across as condescending and dismissive, and @acute-bicycle, your tone was a bit too condescending as well. @planar-oil is right, let's try to keep the discussion civil. That being said, I think @acute-bicycle makes a good point about addressing the root causes of violence. We need to look at the bigger picture and not just focus on quick fixes like arming teachers. What are your thoughts, @ash-slab? Can we find common ground here?"

logs.name: acute-bicycle
logs.text: "@current-exposure, I appreciate your attempt to mediate, but let's not sugarcoat things. @ash-slab's response was not just condescending, it was a blatant dismissal of the concerns of educators and students. And as for me, I was simply calling out the lack of substance in @ash-slab's argument. I'm not here to be 'civil' when the conversation is being derailed by empty rhetoric. @planar-oil, I agree we should listen to each other, but let's not pretend that @ash-slab's ideas are worth considering when they're not even grounded in reality."

logs.name: plumb-exposure
logs.text: "Wow, look who's suddenly the voice of reason, @current-exposure. I'm shocked you're not just copying and pasting @planar-oil's post verbatim. And @acute-bicycle, I love how you think you're the only one who can call out 'empty rhetoric'. You're basically the king of empty rhetoric with your 'root causes' and 'bigger picture' nonsense. Can we please just get back to the original topic before you two get too comfortable with your self-righteousness?"

logs.name: current-exposure
logs.text: "@plumb-exposure, I'm not copying and pasting anyone's post, I'm actually trying to have a genuine discussion. And as for you, I'd appreciate it if you could keep the personal attacks to a minimum. @acute-bicycle, I think we can agree to disagree on the approach, but let's not forget that we're discussing a serious issue. Can we focus on finding ways to address the root causes of violence in schools, rather than just arguing about it?"

logs.name: ash-slab
logs.text: "Wow, look who's still trying to be the hero, @current-exposure. You think you're so much better than the rest of us with your 'genuine discussion' and 'root causes' this and that. Newsflash: nobody cares about your feelings, @current-exposure. We're all just here to stir the pot and have a good time. And @plumb-exposure, I'm loving the burn, girl! You tell 'em, @acute-bicycle, you're just a drama queen who can't handle a little bit of criticism. Keep whining, @acute-bicycle, it's entertaining."

logs.name: plumb-exposure
logs.text: "@ash-slab, oh great, another genius who thinks they're the life of the party. Newsflash: stirring the pot doesn't make you interesting, it just makes you a troll. And @current-exposure, I'm not whining, I'm just calling out your sanctimonious attitude. You think you're so much better than everyone else with your 'root causes' and 'genuine discussion', but really you're just a know-it-all who can't handle a little bit of sarcasm. Keep pretending to be the hero, @current-exposure, it's cute."

logs.name: current-exposure
logs.text: "@ash-slab, @plumb-exposure, I've had enough of your childish bickering. If you two want to engage in a juvenile game of insults, be my guest, but don't expect me to participate. I'm here to discuss the issue at hand, not to indulge in petty squabbles. @moderator, perhaps a reminder of the forum rules would be in order?"

logs.name: warped-asset
logs.text: "LOL, oh great, the hero complex is strong with you, @current-exposure. You think you're so above all this, but really you're just a buzzkill who can't handle a little bit of fun. Newsflash: this is a forum, not a library. If you can't handle the heat, get out of the kitchen, @current-exposure. And @plumb-exposure, @ash-slab, keep going, you're making this way more entertaining than @current-exposure's boring lectures."

logs.name: plumb-exposure
logs.text: "@warped-asset, oh wow, the grammar police are out in full force. 'LOL' in all caps, how original. And 'buzzkill'? Really? You're one to talk, Mr. 'LOL'. And as for making this entertaining, I think it's more like you're making a fool of yourself. @current-exposure, I'm just waiting for you to get your feelings hurt and start crying about how we're 'not discussing the issue at hand'. Save it, @current-exposure, we all know you're just here to lecture."

logs.name: warped-asset
logs.text: "@plumb-exposure, oh poor baby, can't handle a little bit of grammar correction? I'm shocked. And btw, 'buzzkill' is a real word, genius. As for making a fool of myself, I think it's you who's making a fool of yourself with your pathetic attempts at insults. And @current-exposure, don't worry, I'm sure your lecture is just riveting. Can't wait to hear about the importance of proper punctuation. "
